name: Muhammad ibn Abdullah
title: Memory of Tayyar
shortDescription: Awn's brother and another brave son of Lady Zaynab (SA) who was martyred alongside his brother defending Wilayah.
fullStory:
Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Ja'far was the full brother of Awn and another brave son of Lady Zaynab (SA). Following his father Abdullah's advice, he too traveled from Medina and Mecca alongside his noble uncle, Imam Hussain (AS). The presence of these two young brothers in the caravan demonstrated the deep insight and devotion of Lady Zaynab's family to Wilayah.

Throughout the journey from Mecca to Karbala, Muhammad constantly served the Imam and the Ahl al-Bayt alongside his brother Awn. Bravery boiled in his veins, inherited from Ja'far al-Tayyar (the conqueror of Mu'tah) and Ali ibn Abi Talib (AS). He eagerly awaited the day to prove his loyalty in action.

After the martyrdom of his brother Awn on the day of Ashura, Muhammad immediately and without hesitation entered the battlefield to avenge his brother and defend the sanctity of the Imamate. Like his brother, he recited epic war poetry and struck terror into the hearts of the Kufan army with his sword blows.

Sheikh Mufid narrates in Al-Irshad that Muhammad recited this poetry on the battlefield: 'I complain to God of the transgressing enemies; a group blindly deviating from the right path...'. He shattered the enemy ranks with his successive attacks, displaying a heroic fight.

Finally, after a fierce battle, Amir ibn Nahshal al-Tamimi attacked him and dealt a heavy blow, martyring him. With Muhammad's martyrdom, Lady Zaynab (SA) sacrificed two of her dearest beloved children in one day and on one path for her brother Imam Hussain (AS), proving that love for Wilayah transcends even love for one's children.
quoteArabic: أَشْكُو إِلَى اللَّهِ مِنَ العُدْوانِ، فِعْلَ قَوْمٍ عُمُيٍ عَنِ الرُّشْدانِ
quote: I complain to God of the transgressing enemies; a group blindly deviating from the right path.
